questions.text: The distance moved by an object in unit time is called its:
The basic unit of time is:
questions.explanation: While minutes and hours are used, the SI base unit for time is the second (s).

questions.text: A motion that repeats itself at regular intervals of time is:
questions.explanation: Examples include the swinging of a pendulum or the motion of the earth around the sun.

questions.text: The time taken by a pendulum to complete one oscillation is its:
questions.explanation: The time period of a simple pendulum is constant for a given length.

questions.text: Which device is used to measure the speed of a vehicle?
questions.explanation: The speedometer records the speed directly in km/h.

questions.text: The distance-time graph for an object moving with a constant speed is a:
questions.explanation: A straight line starting from the origin indicates uniform motion.
An odometer tracks the total distance covered by the vehicle.

questions.text: Which of these is the correct relationship?
questions.explanation: This formula allows us to calculate any of the three variables if the other two are known.

questions.text: A car travels 100 km in 2 hours. What is its speed?

questions.explanation: Before digital clocks, natural periodic events and these ancient devices were used to track time.
